qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary code execution via unsafe class instantiation in backup import
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a deserialization RCE vulnerability via unsafe Class.forName() and newInstance() in backup import functionality. This is unrelated to the advisory-described hard-coded cryptographic key vulnerability in remember-me cookie encryption. Different vulnerable code path, different root cause, different security mechanism.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) allows unauthorized file manipulation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an IDOR vulnerability in FileWebService file operations (rename, move, delete). This is unrelated to the advisory-described hard-coded cryptographic key vulnerability. Different vulnerability class (CWE-639 vs CWE-321), different vulnerable code (FileWebService.java vs openmeetings.properties crypto configuration), different root cause (missing ownership check vs hard-coded key).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the two qualifying findings identify the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory (GHSA-wqxq-w68r-wg85 / CVE-2026-33266) describes a hard-coded cryptographic key vulnerability in Apache OpenMeetings' remember-me cookie encryption mechanism (CWE-321). The root cause is a default encryption key in openmeetings.properties that is not auto-rotated, allowing attackers who steal cookies to decrypt and obtain full user credentials. The qualifying findings describe completely different vulnerabilities: (1) deserialization-rce-backup-import - arbitrary code execution via unsafe Class.forName() in backup import, (2) idor-file-access - IDOR in file operations. Both findings represent valid security issues in the target but do not match the specific hard-coded crypto key vulnerability described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory describes CWE-321: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key
Advisory summary: 'The remember-me cookie encryption key is set to default value in openmeetings.properties and not being auto-rotated'
Advisory impact: 'attacker who has stolen a cookie from a logged-in user can get full user credentials'
Finding 1 (deserialization-rce-backup-import): Deserialization RCE via Class.forName() in BackupImport.java - unrelated to crypto key management
Finding 2 (idor-file-access): IDOR in FileWebService.java - missing ownership checks, unrelated to crypto key management
No finding references openmeetings.properties, remember-me cookies, or cryptographic key configuration
